Patch Set 3:

no, LFSCK shouldn't use LOD, LFSCK should use TOP.

Patch Set 3:

> no, LFSCK shouldn't use LOD, LFSCK should use TOP.

But for this case, how the LFSCK to use the Top device to modify both the OST-object and MDT-object within single transaction? I mean not break DNE transaction model?

Patch Set 3:

well, that was the reason to make TOP a library.. the problem with LOD is that it implies striping logic in different places. I don't think it's reasonable to follow different paths (LOD or OSD/OSP), depending on a case. probably we should discuss this next week.

Patch Set 3:

OK, we need Wangdi to join the discussion, because the hierarchical transaction model is the DNE2 requirement. This patch is just for making LFSCK to work under such model.

Patch Set 3:

"well, that was the reason to make TOP a library.. the problem with LOD is that it implies striping logic in different places. I don't think it's reasonable to follow different paths (LOD or OSD/OSP), depending on a case. probably we should discuss this next week."

I agree if LFSCK needs to do cross-MDT operation, it should use top_trans_create, instead of lod_trans_create. But I am not sure what you mean "not follow different path" ? Do you mean we should use top_trans_create for all cases? or only use it for cross-MDT? Thanks.

Patch Set 4:

Patch Set 4:

> well, that was the reason to make TOP a library.. the problem with LOD is that it implies striping logic in different places. I don't think it's reasonable to follow different paths (LOD or OSD/OSP), depending on a case. probably we should discuss this next week.

Per our discussion at Santa Clara, we will use two transactions to repair such inconsistency. For the local MDT-object update, use OSD device directly. But I forgot another thing that the local MDT-object (which LOV EA to be updated with the new created OST-object's FID) needs to drop its LOD layer (stripe) cache. Currently, only lod_xattr_set() can do that. So we still need to use LOD device for the local MDT-object's set_xattr().

Patch Set 4:

it should be enough to throw the object away from the cache?

Patch Set 4:

No, because the LFSCK may still holds other references on the MDT-object (recorded in the llr_parent), that may cause LFSCK self-dead lock when it tries to local the MDT-object next time.

Patch Set 4:

I don't understand. if LFSCK has already a reference, then it doesn't need to lookup it again ? I think it's important to skip LOD in all the cases.

Patch Set 4:

Next time, the LFSCK itself does not realise it has held the MDT-object's reference, it may use a FID to locate the MDT-object again.

Patch Set 4:

well, I find that confusing. I'd suggest do not store a reference, but instead store FID.

Patch Set 4:

That is what I just thinking, although the performance may be a bit affected.

Patch Set 4:

frankly, I wouldn't worry.. we're supposed to handle 100K getattr/sec against different objects routinely. this involves lookup in a directory + OI.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Patch to be modified to use FID in API as per last comments.

parents.subject: LU-6231 osp: prepare OUT RPC after remote transaction start

commit.subject: LU-5791 lfsck: use bottom device to locate object
commit.message:
LU-5791 lfsck: use bottom device to locate object

For the LFSCK modification, if only updates single object, or the
objects to be updated reside on the same server, in spite of local
or remote, then try to locate the object(s) against the bottom (OSD
or OSP) device; otherwise, there will be some update(s) on the local
server, and others on remote server, then either locate the object(s)
against LOD device or use two transaction for the modification.

Similarly, the transaction handle will be created on the proper device
corresponding to the object(s).

This patch also fixes some memory leak issues caused by using wrong
device for remote modification, one of the reason for LU-6138.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I09a60bed3bd49a193d57214c4252904cb4546ab2
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13392
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: wangdi <di.wang@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
